title: Authors and Citation
text: Guillaume Noblet. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Noblet G (2026). AddFonts: Download Register Fonts GDPR-Compliant Providers Use R Graphics. R package version 1.0.0, https://codeberg.org/gnoblet/AddFonts.
code: @Manual{,   title = {AddFonts: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics},   author = {Guillaume Noblet},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://codeberg.org/gnoblet/AddFonts}, }

what: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: Download register fonts R multiple sources: Bunny Fonts (GDPR-compliant CDN), local files, direct URLs, custom file-based providers. Fonts cached first use registered via sysfonts work immediately showtext ggplot2.

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: 🔒 Privacy-first default: Bunny Fonts CDN — tracking, GDPR-compliant 📂 Multiple sources: CDN providers, local files, direct URLs, custom providers 💾 Smart caching: Downloads/copies , reuses across R sessions 🎨 Full variant support: regular, bold, italic, bold-italic

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
code: # From r-universe install.packages("AddFonts", repos = "https://gnoblet.r-universe.dev")

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: Requires woff2 command-line tool convert fonts:
code: # Debian/Ubuntu sudo apt install woff2  # Fedora/RHEL sudo dnf install woff2-tools  # Arch Linux sudo pacman -S woff2  # macOS brew install woff2  # Windows or else # Compile from: https://github.com/google/woff2

what: Add Fonts With One Core Function
title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: add font R, simply call add_font() font family name retrieved Bunny Fonts website. example, add “Oswald” font:
code: library(AddFonts) # Download and register Oswald font add_font("oswald") #> ✔ Converted '/home/gnoblet/.cache/AddFonts/bunny-oswald-latin-400-normal.woff2' to TTF: '/home/gnoblet/.cache/AddFonts/bunny-oswald-latin-400-normal.ttf' #> ✔ Downloaded variant: 'bunny-oswald-latin-400-normal.ttf' #> ✔ Converted '/home/gnoblet/.cache/AddFonts/bunny-oswald-latin-700-normal.woff2' to TTF: '/home/gnoblet/.cache/AddFonts/bunny-oswald-latin-700-normal.ttf' #> ✔ Downloaded variant: 'bunny-oswald-latin-700-normal.ttf' #> ✔ Font "oswald" registered and added to cache.

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: preview_font() function plots downloaded registered variants font family one go. picked font, instance “Merriweather”, calling preview_font() function show variants, “Merriweather” 4: regular, bold, italic, bold-italic.
code: library(AddFonts) preview_font("merriweather", regular.wt = 400, bold.wt = 700)

what: Add Fonts And Use With ggplot2
title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: fonts registered, can used ggplot2 via showtext. download three fonts display plot:
code: library(AddFonts) library(showtext) library(ggplot2)  fonts <- c("oswald", "merriweather", "playfair-display")  for (font in fonts) {   add_font(font) } showtext_auto()  font_data <- data.frame(   x = 0.5,   y = seq(0.75, 0.25, length.out = length(fonts)),   label = paste(tools::toTitleCase(gsub("-", " ", fonts)), "Font"),   family = fonts,   size = c(9, 8, 8) )  ggplot(   font_data,   aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, family = family, size = size) ) +   geom_text(hjust = 0.5) +   scale_size_identity() +   xlim(0, 1) +   ylim(0, 1) +   theme_void()

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: Fonts stored user-level cache directory. Use functions inspect clear cache need free disk space force re-download.
code: # Show cache location get_cache_dir()  # Remove a specific font cache_clean(families = c("oswald"))  # Clear everything cache_clean(reset = TRUE)

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: add_font() supports several font sources via provider argument.

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: default provider. Supply font family name appears fonts.bunny.net:
code: add_font("oswald")

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: Pass absolute paths TTF OTF files already disk:
code: add_font(   "my-font",   provider = "file",   variants = list(     regular = "/path/to/MyFont-Regular.ttf",     bold = "/path/to/MyFont-Bold.ttf"   ) )

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: Download URL — provider account needed:
code: add_font(   "my-font",   provider = "url",   variants = list(     regular = "https://example.com/fonts/MyFont-Regular.ttf"   ) )

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: Use FontProviderFile() CDN providers serve static font files (e.g. Bye Bye Binary):
code: bbb <- FontProviderFile(   source = "bbb",   base_url = "https://bye.bye.binary.com/fonts/{family}/{filename}.ttf" )  add_font(   "alpaga",   provider = bbb,   variants = list(regular = "Alpaga-Regular", bold = "Alpaga-Bold") )

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: Bunny Fonts default provider mirrors Google Fonts tracking data collection — important EU users privacy-conscious projects. fast global CDN free open-source. font sources, see Providers section .

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: add_font() follows three steps every provider: Check cache — font already disk, register immediately return Fetch — download CDN, copy local path, fetch URL; Bunny Fonts, also converts WOFF2 → TTF (R’s sysfonts requires TTF format) Register — calls sysfonts::font_add() font available graphics devices via showtext background, S7 classes manage provider metadata cache entries.

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: showtext - Using fonts R graphics sysfonts - Loading fonts R S7 - new OO system R see also pyfonts registering fonts Python (made Joseph Barbier).

title: Download and Register Fonts from GDPR-Compliant Providers for Use in R Graphics
text: GPL (>= 3) — see LICENSE

title: S7-backed cache entry (CacheEntry) — CacheEntry
text: S7-backed cache entry (CacheEntry)

title: S7-backed cache entry (CacheEntry) — CacheEntry
code: CacheEntry(family = character(0), meta = CacheMeta())

title: S7-backed cache entry (CacheEntry) — CacheEntry
text: family (character(1)) Family name cache entry (safe identifier containing letters, digits, hyphens). meta (CacheMeta) CacheMeta object describing cached files origin family.

title: S7-backed cache entry (CacheEntry) — CacheEntry
text: (S7_object) validated S7 CacheEntry object.

what: S7 list of cache entries (CacheEntryList) — CacheEntryList
title: S7 list of cache entries (CacheEntryList) — CacheEntryList
text: S7 list cache entries (CacheEntryList)

title: S7 list of cache entries (CacheEntryList) — CacheEntryList
code: CacheEntryList(entries = list())

title: S7 list of cache entries (CacheEntryList) — CacheEntryList
text: entries (list(1+)) non-empty list CacheEntry objects.

title: S7 list of cache entries (CacheEntryList) — CacheEntryList
text: (S7_object) validated S7 CacheEntryList object.

title: S7-backed cache metadata (CacheMeta) — CacheMeta
text: S7-backed cache metadata (CacheMeta)

title: S7-backed cache metadata (CacheMeta) — CacheMeta
code: CacheMeta(source, files, key_scheme = NULL, failed_keys = character(0))

title: S7-backed cache metadata (CacheMeta) — CacheMeta
text: source (character(1)) Name provider source produced cached font files. files (list(1+)) non-empty named list file paths. Names must follow scheme declared key_scheme. (default: NULL) key_scheme (character(1)) Key scheme used files: "weight" numeric weight keys (e.g. "400", "700italic") "symbolic" variant keys ("regular", "bold", etc.). failed_keys (character(0+)) character vector keys requested failed download. Empty requested keys successfully downloaded. (default: character(0))

title: S7-backed cache metadata (CacheMeta) — CacheMeta
text: (S7_object) validated S7 CacheMeta object.

title: Base font provider class (FontProvider) — FontProvider
text: Abstract base class shared provider types. construct directly. Use FontProviderWeight() FontProviderFile() instead.

title: Base font provider class (FontProvider) — FontProvider
code: FontProvider(   source = character(0),   aliases = list(),   first_use_message = character(0),   first_use_url = character(0) )

title: Base font provider class (FontProvider) — FontProvider
text: source (character(1)) Provider id/name (e.g. "bunny", "bbb"). aliases (list) Optional list alias strings recognise provider (e.g. list("fonts.bunny.net")). first_use_message (character(1) | NULL) Optional message displayed per R session first time provider used (e.g. licensing notice). NULL means message. first_use_url (character(1) | NULL) Optional URL shown alongside first_use_message. NULL means URL.

title: Base font provider class (FontProvider) — FontProvider
text: (FontProvider) S7 base class. Use subclass constructor practice.

what: Sentinel provider for direct-URL font downloads (provider =
title: Sentinel provider for direct-URL font downloads (provider =
text: Returned internally add_font(provider = "url") used. Carries extra properties — type alone signals direct-URL dispatch path.

title: Sentinel provider for direct-URL font downloads (provider =
code: FontProviderDirectURL()

title: File-based font provider (FontProviderFile) — FontProviderFile
text: Provider downloads font files directly filename base URL, weight/style/subset parameterisation. Covers Git-hosted collections like Bye Bye Binary variant fixed filename.

title: File-based font provider (FontProviderFile) — FontProviderFile
code: FontProviderFile(   source,   base_url,   file_ext = "ttf",   aliases = list(),   first_use_message = NULL,   first_use_url = NULL )

title: File-based font provider (FontProviderFile) — FontProviderFile
text: source (character(1)) Provider id/name (e.g. "bbb"). base_url (character(1)) Glue-style URL template. Must contain {family} {filename} placeholders (e.g. "https://gitlab.com/bye-bye-binary/{family}/-/raw/main/ttf/{filename}.ttf"). file_ext (character(1)) Extension font files served provider (default: "ttf"). Must "ttf" "otf" (conversion performed). aliases (list) Optional alias strings (inherited FontProvider()). first_use_message (character(1) | NULL) Inherited FontProvider(). first_use_url (character(1) | NULL) Inherited FontProvider().

title: File-based font provider (FontProviderFile) — FontProviderFile
text: (FontProviderFile) validated S7 FontProviderFile object.

what: Sentinel provider for local font files (provider =
title: Sentinel provider for local font files (provider =
text: Returned internally add_font(provider = "file") used. Carries extra properties — type alone signals local-copy dispatch path.

title: Sentinel provider for local font files (provider =
code: FontProviderLocal()

title: Weight-based font provider (FontProviderWeight) — FontProviderWeight
text: Provider resolves font variants numeric weight style via glue-style URL template. covers APIs like Bunny Fonts.

title: Weight-based font provider (FontProviderWeight) — FontProviderWeight
code: FontProviderWeight(   source,   url_template,   conversion = NULL,   conversion_ext = NULL,   aliases = list(),   first_use_message = NULL,   first_use_url = NULL )

title: Weight-based font provider (FontProviderWeight) — FontProviderWeight
text: source (character(1)) Provider id/name (e.g. "bunny"). url_template (character(1)) Glue-style URL template. Must contain {family}. Typically also uses {subset}, {weight} (integer), {style}. conversion (character(1) | NULL) Name conversion function apply download (e.g. "woff2_to_ttf"), NULL provider serves TTF directly. conversion_ext (character(1) | NULL) File extension downloaded artifact conversion (e.g. "woff2"), NULL. aliases (list) Optional alias strings (inherited FontProvider()). first_use_message (character(1) | NULL) Inherited FontProvider(). first_use_url (character(1) | NULL) Inherited FontProvider().

title: Weight-based font provider (FontProviderWeight) — FontProviderWeight
text: (FontProviderWeight) validated S7 FontProviderWeight object.

what: Add a font to the local cache and register it for use — add_font
title: Add a font to the local cache and register it for use — add_font
text: Ensure font available locally: try cache first, otherwise download/convert register font can used plotting devices. Returns (invisibly) list local file paths.

title: Add a font to the local cache and register it for use — add_font
code: add_font(   name,   provider = "bunny",   family = NULL,   variants = NULL,   regular.wt = 400,   bold.wt = 700,   subset = "latin" )

title: Add a font to the local cache and register it for use — add_font
text: name (character(1)) Name font known provider. provider (character(1) | FontProvider) Provider id/name (default: "bunny"), FontProvider object constructed FontProviderWeight() FontProviderFile() (bypasses registry lookup). family (character | NULL) Optional family name register font (default: NULL). variants (list | NULL) file-based providers . Named list mapping symbolic variant keys ("regular", "italic", "bold", "bolditalic") filename stems served provider (without extension). Must include least "regular". Ignored weight-based providers (default: NULL). regular.wt (numeric(1)) weight-based providers. Regular weight request (default: 400). bold.wt (numeric(1)) weight-based providers. Bold weight request (default: 700). subset (character(1)) weight-based providers. Glyph subset request (default: "latin").

title: Add a font to the local cache and register it for use — add_font
text: (list) Invisibly returns list paths regular, italic, bold bolditalic variants, throws error failure.

title: Add a font to the local cache and register it for use — add_font
text: weight-based providers (e.g. Bunny Fonts), supply regular.wt,bold.wt, subset. file-based providers (e.g. Bye Bye Binary), supply variants instead.

text: (character | NULL) Invisibly returns character vector removed family names deleting specific entries, NULL nothing changed. Remove files default.

Defaults get_cache_dir() NULL.

title: Build a CacheEntry, persist the cache index, and return the entry — .persist_cache_entry

Empty requested keys successfully downloaded. (default: character(0))

what: Preview a font by ensuring it's installed and drawing a sample string — preview_font
title: Preview a font by ensuring it's installed and drawing a sample string — preview_font
text: Ensure requested font installed via add_font() draw brief sample using showtext proper font rendering.

title: Preview a font by ensuring it's installed and drawing a sample string — preview_font
code: preview_font(   name,   provider = "bunny",   family = NULL,   variants = NULL,   sample = "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog",   size = 28,   subset = "latin",   regular.wt = 400,   bold.wt = 700 )

title: Preview a font by ensuring it's installed and drawing a sample string — preview_font
text: name (character(1)) Font name used provider (e.g. "oswald"). provider (character(1)) Provider name use (default: "bunny") family (character | NULL) Optional family name register font (default: NULL) variants (list | NULL) file-based providers (provider = "file", provider = "url", FontProviderFile object). Named list symbolic variant keys file paths / URLs / filename stems. Passed add_font(). Ignored weight-based providers (default: NULL). sample (character(1)) Sample text display (default: "quick brown fox jumps lazy dog") size (numeric(1)) Font size points preview (default: 28) subset (character(1)) Glyph subset request (default: "latin") regular.wt (integer(1)) Regular weight display (default: 400) bold.wt (integer(1)) Bold weight display (default: 700)

title: Preview a font by ensuring it's installed and drawing a sample string — preview_font
text: (list) Invisibly returns list paths produced add_font().

title: Validate a cache entry and register the font with sysfonts if the required files exist. Returns the prepared files list or NULL when registration cannot proceed. — register_from_cache
text: (list | NULL) Prepared files list (regular, italic, bold, bolditalic) NULL.

text: entry (CacheEntry) Existing cache entry update. provider (FontProvider) Provider object used downloads. name (character(1)) Font name provider. family_name (character(1)) Family name font. missing_weights (numeric) Vector weights download add cache. subset (character(1)) Glyph subset request (default: "latin"). cache_dir (character | NULL) Cache directory use. cel (CacheEntryList) Current cache entry list update.

what: Convert a .woff2 font to .ttf using the system 'woff2_decompress' tool — woff2_to_ttf
title: Convert a .woff2 font to .ttf using the system 'woff2_decompress' tool — woff2_to_ttf
text: Internal helper. Uses system woff2_decompress tool convert .woff2 file .ttf file.

title: Convert a .woff2 font to .ttf using the system 'woff2_decompress' tool — woff2_to_ttf
code: woff2_to_ttf(font_file, overwrite = FALSE, remove_old = TRUE, quiet = FALSE)

title: Convert a .woff2 font to .ttf using the system 'woff2_decompress' tool — woff2_to_ttf
text: font_file (character(1)) Path .woff2 file convert. overwrite (logical(1)) TRUE, overwrite existing .ttf conversion. remove_old (logical(1)) TRUE, remove original .woff2 file conversion try. quiet (logical(1)) TRUE, suppress success message. Errors always abort regardless setting (default: FALSE).

title: Convert a .woff2 font to .ttf using the system 'woff2_decompress' tool — woff2_to_ttf
text: (character(1)) Invisibly returns path .ttf file success.

title: Social Media Posts for AddFonts Launch
text: 🚀 Excited share first experimental R package: AddFonts! built scratch itch - downloading registering GDPR-compliant fonts R simple. AddFonts uses Bunny Fonts (privacy-first alternative Google Fonts) add beautiful typography plots just one function call. Fair warning: much rough version - experimental subject evolving. works! made fun: - 🎓 Learning S7 (https://github.com/RConsortium/S7) - new object system R. Great modeling font metadata cache logic - 📦 Using rv (https://a2-ai.github.io/rv-docs/) - declarative dependency management actually makes sense - 🌐 Publishing via r-universe (https://gnoblet.r-universe.dev/AddFonts) - painless package distribution - 🔒 Prioritizing privacy Bunny Fonts (tracking, fully GDPR-compliant) result: Smart caching, full variant support (regular/bold/italic), one-line setup. ’re R graphics space care GDPR compliance, ’d love feedback! make useful ? Install: install.packages("AddFonts", repos = "https://gnoblet.r-universe.dev") Docs: http://guillaume-noblet.com/AddFonts/ #RStats #DataViz #OpenSource #Privacy #GDPR
code: library(AddFonts) add_font("merriweather")  # That's it!

title: Social Media Posts for AddFonts Launch
text: 🎨 Just launched AddFonts - first experimental R pkg! One function download & register GDPR-compliant fonts Bunny Fonts. tracking, just beautiful typography. ’s rough around edges (v0.1!), works. fun playing S7 classes & rv package manager. Feedback welcome! 📦 install.packages(“AddFonts”, repos = “https://gnoblet.r-universe.dev”) 📚 guillaume-noblet.com/AddFonts/ #RStats #DataViz
code: library(AddFonts) add_font("oswald")

title: Social Media Posts for AddFonts Launch
text: learned building AddFonts: ✨ S7 classes actually nice modeling font metadata 📦 rv makes R dependency management declarative (finally!) 🌐 r-universe makes package distribution painless 🔒 Bunny Fonts = Google Fonts without tracking 🎯 WOFF2→TTF conversion easier expected Still experimental, shipping beats perfecting. github.com/RConsortium/S7 a2-ai.github.io/rv-docs/ r-universe.dev #RStats #LearnInPublic

title: Social Media Posts for AddFonts Launch
text: Privacy-conscious data viz folks: AddFonts lets use 1400+ fonts R plots via Bunny Fonts (GDPR-compliant, zero tracking). Downloads , caches forever. Perfect EU projects anyone values privacy. ⚠️ Experimental! functional. #RStats #Privacy #GDPR
code: add_font("merriweather") preview_font("merriweather")

title: Social Media Posts for AddFonts Launch
text: 🎨 Built first R package: AddFonts Privacy-first fonts R graphics via Bunny Fonts. One function, smart caching, GDPR-compliant. ⚠️ experimental v0.1 ✨ Powered S7 & rv 🔒 tracking, 1400+ fonts install.packages(“AddFonts”, repos = “https://gnoblet.r-universe.dev”) #RStats #DataViz

title: AddFonts 1.0.0
text: Stable release. Minimum R version lowered 4.1.0 (4.5.0). Failed font weight downloads now recorded cache avoid repeated retry attempts subsequent calls (#16). Cleaned exports: user-facing functions now exported (add_font, cache_clean, preview_font, list_providers, register_provider, unregister_provider, FontProviderFile, FontProviderWeight). Internal S7 classes longer exported (#9). Internal refactoring: pure S7 dispatch add_font(), CacheMeta gains key_scheme property, sentinel provider types replace runtime detection.

title: AddFonts 0.1.0
text: Rough draft release.
